title: Runtime Event Schema + Persistence Layer
consumer_purpose: Persist all bus events to SQLite for audit, replay, and debugging
claim_atoms: runtime_events SQLite table created via Drizzle
Bus→EventStore bridge subscribes to all bus events
content:
## Files to create

### 1. packages/opencode/src/event/runtime-event.ts
- Define RuntimeEvent schema using Effect Schema (Schema.Struct)
- Fields: id, sessionId, runId, parentEventId?, correlationId?, ts, actor (literal union), eventType, phase?, status?, toolName?, filePath?, model?, durationMs?, tokenInput?, tokenOutput?, errorCode?, errorMessage?, recoverable?, payloadJson?
- actor is literal union: "user" | "assistant" | "tool" | "system" | "lifecycle"
- Use Schema.String, Schema.Number, Schema.Boolean, Schema.optional, Schema.optionalNullable

### 2. packages/opencode/src/event/event.sql.ts
- runtime_events table using sqliteTable from drizzle-orm/sqlite-core
- snake_case columns matching RuntimeEvent fields
- Indexes on session_id, event_type, ts, actor
- Use $type<T>() for typed columns
- payload_json stored as text({ mode: "json" })

### 3. packages/opencode/src/event/event-store.ts
- EventStore interface with record(event: RuntimeEvent) and query(filters) methods
- Use DatabaseAdapter.Service for DB access via Effect
- Implement insert for record
- Implement query with WHERE clause building for filters

### 4. packages/opencode/src/event/event-bridge.ts
- Layer that subscribes to all bus events via Bus.Service.subscribeAll()
- Transforms each Payload into RuntimeEvent
- Records via EventStore
- Runs as background fiber (Effect.forkScoped) in a Layer.scoped

### 5. packages/opencode/src/event/index.ts
- Re-export everything from all modules

## Files to modify

### 6. packages/opencode/src/storage/schema.ts
- Add `export { RuntimeEventTable } from "../event/event.sql"`

## Important patterns
- Use Effect.gen for all effectful code
- Use Context.Tag for service tags
- Use Layer.effect / Layer.scoped for layers
- Use existing Log utility
- Follow snake_case for Drizzle columns
- Use text({ mode: "json" }) for JSON payload field
